You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). You have Bash, Read, Grep tools.

STEP 1 — get your file list. Run:
  /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; s=json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[1]; print(chr(10).join(s))"
That prints your assigned slice as lines of "relpath:START-END" (relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent).

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every one of your assigned files/ranges, line by line, using the Read tool with the given offset/limit. Do NOT grep-and-skim. Actually read the code so you catch names a keyword search would miss.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (module/file name, class, function/method, variable, constant, enum value) that would look UNPROFESSIONAL in an external code review. This includes:
  - marketing / quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, proper(when boastful), aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best, clean(when boastful)
  - temporal / version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(when it means 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes / informal / vague: hack, yolo, stuff, misc, foo, doit, thing, blah, magic numbers named as words
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME, not KEEP.** Only mark KEEP for names that are unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a real domain term (e.g. 'finalize', 'proper_noun', 'evict_older_than', a real product name like 'SmartArt' ONLY if the code truly implements it).

STEP 4 — WRITE your findings by APPENDING TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_1.tsv, one row per flagged name:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY_it_reads_unprofessional<TAB>SUGGESTED_professional_name
  (VERDICT = RENAME or KEEP-BUT-NOTED; KIND = file/class/func/var/const/enum)
Use a bash heredoc or printf with real tab characters. If a file range has NO issues, still write one row: NONE<TAB>-<TAB><relpath><TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-.

Return ONLY a one-line summary: "shard 1: <R> renames, <K> kept-noted across <F> files".

- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""
2	Static import-closure analysis to identify live vs orphaned code.
3	
4	From live entry points (orchestrators, passing tests, smoke tests),
5	compute the transitive set of imported modules. Anything under src/
6	or scripts/ that is NOT reached is a candidate for archive.
7	
8	Produces: docs/live_code_audit.md (markdown report).
9	
10	No files are moved or deleted — this is read-only analysis.
11	"""
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import ast
15	import json
16	import os
17	import subprocess
18	import sys
19	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
20	from pathlib import Path
21	
22	
23	ROOT = Path(r"C:/POLARIS")
24	SRC = ROOT / "src"
25	SCRIPTS = ROOT / "scripts"
26	TESTS = ROOT / "tests"
27	
28	
29	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
30	# Entry points — files whose imports (direct + transitive) count as "live".
31	# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
32	def _find_entry_points() -> list[Path]:
33	    """Every script with `if __name__ == "__main__":` is an entry point,
34	    plus every test file. This maximally widens the 'live' set so we
35	    don't falsely accuse tools the user runs directly."""
36	    eps: list[Path] = []
37	    # All __main__-scripts (under scripts/ only; src/ shouldn't have them)
38	    for p in SCRIPTS.rglob("*.py"):
39	        if "__pycache__" in p.parts:
40	            continue
41	        try:
42	            txt = p.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
43	        except OSError:
44	            continue
45	        if 'if __name__ == "__main__":' in txt or "if __name__ == '__main__':" in txt:
46	            eps.append(p)
47	    # All tests
48	    for p in TESTS.rglob("test_*.py"):
49	        eps.append(p)
50	    return eps
51	
52	
53	ENTRY_POINTS: list[Path] = _find_entry_points()
54	
55	
56	@dataclass
57	class ModuleInfo:
58	    path: Path
59	    imports: set[str] = field(default_factory=set)
60	
61	
62	def module_name_for_path(path: Path) -> str:
63	    """Convert /abs/src/polaris_graph/x/y.py -> src.polaris_graph.x.y"""
64	    rel = path.relative_to(ROOT).with_suffix("")
65	    parts = rel.parts
66	    if parts and parts[-1] == "__init__":
67	        parts = parts[:-1]
68	    return ".".join(parts)
69	
70	
71	def extract_imports(path: Path) -> set[str]:
72	    """Return the fully-qualified modules imported by this file."""
73	    try:
74	        source = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
75	    except OSError:
76	        return set()
77	    try:
78	        tree = ast.parse(source, filename=str(path))
79	    except SyntaxError:
80	        return set()
81	    imports: set[str] = set()
82	    for node in ast.walk(tree):
83	        if isinstance(node, ast.Import):
84	            for alias in node.names:
85	                imports.add(alias.name)
86	        elif isinstance(node, ast.ImportFrom):
87	            if node.module:
88	                # Handle relative imports by resolving against package
89	                if node.level and node.level > 0:
90	                    # Relative import — need to resolve
91	                    pkg = module_name_for_path(path)
92	                    pkg_parts = pkg.split(".")
93	                    if node.level >= len(pkg_parts):
94	                        continue
95	                    base = ".".join(pkg_parts[: -node.level + 1] if node.level > 1
96	                                     else pkg_parts[:-0 or None])
97	                    # Simpler: rebuild absolute path
98	                    anchor = pkg_parts[:-node.level]
99	                    full = ".".join(anchor + [node.module]) if anchor else node.module
100	                    imports.add(full)
101	                else:
102	                    imports.add(node.module)
103	                    for alias in node.names:
104	                        # Also record sub-import (from x import y may reach x.y module)
105	                        imports.add(f"{node.module}.{alias.name}")
106	            else:
107	                # from . import x
108	                pkg = module_name_for_path(path)
109	                pkg_parts = pkg.split(".")
110	                if node.level <= len(pkg_parts):
111	                    anchor = pkg_parts[:-node.level] if node.level > 0 else pkg_parts
112	                    for alias in node.names:
113	                        imports.add(".".join(anchor + [alias.name]))
114	    return imports
115	
116	
117	def scan_all_python_files(base: Path) -> dict[Path, ModuleInfo]:
118	    """Parse every .py under `base` and return path -> ModuleInfo."""
119	    out: dict[Path, ModuleInfo] = {}
120	    for p in base.rglob("*.py"):
121	        if "__pycache__" in p.parts:
122	            continue
123	        out[p.resolve()] = ModuleInfo(path=p, imports=extract_imports(p))
124	    return out
125	
126	
127	def build_module_index(all_files: dict[Path, ModuleInfo]) -> dict[str, Path]:
128	    """Map module name (fully qualified) -> file path."""
129	    idx: dict[str, Path] = {}
130	    for path in all_files:
131	        name = module_name_for_path(path)
132	        idx[name] = path
133	    return idx
134	
135	
136	def resolve_import_to_path(
137	    imp: str, module_index: dict[str, Path],
138	) -> Path | None:
139	    """Given an import string like 'src.polaris_graph.generator.x',
140	    return the path to that module, or None if not a project file."""
141	    # Exact match
142	    if imp in module_index:
143	        return module_index[imp]
144	    # Strip trailing component (for `from x.y import Z` where Z is a name, not a module)
145	    parts = imp.split(".")
146	    while len(parts) > 1:
147	        parts = parts[:-1]
148	        candidate = ".".join(parts)
149	        if candidate in module_index:
150	            return module_index[candidate]
151	    return None
152	
153	
154	def compute_live_closure(
155	    entry_points: list[Path],
156	    all_files: dict[Path, ModuleInfo],
157	    module_index: dict[str, Path],
158	) -> set[Path]:
159	    """Transitive closure of imports from entry points."""
160	    live: set[Path] = set()
161	    queue: list[Path] = []
162	    for ep in entry_points:
163	        ep_res = ep.resolve()
164	        if ep_res in all_files:
165	            queue.append(ep_res)
166	            live.add(ep_res)
167	    while queue:
168	        current = queue.pop()
169	        info = all_files.get(current)
170	        if info is None:
171	            continue
172	        for imp in info.imports:
173	            target = resolve_import_to_path(imp, module_index)
174	            if target is not None and target not in live:
175	                live.add(target)
176	                queue.append(target)
177	    return live
178	
179	
180	def git_last_commit_date(path: Path) -> str:
181	    try:
182	        result = subprocess.run(
183	            ["git", "-C", str(ROOT), "log", "-1", "--format=%cd",
184	             "--date=short", "--", str(path.relative_to(ROOT))],
185	            capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5,
186	        )
187	        return result.stdout.strip() or "never"
188	    except Exception:
189	        return "error"
190	
191	
192	def first_docstring(path: Path) -> str:
193	    try:
194	        tree = ast.parse(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"))
195	        doc = ast.get_docstring(tree)
196	        if doc:
197	            first_line = doc.strip().split("\n")[0]
198	            return first_line[:120]
199	    except Exception:
200	        pass
201	    return ""
202	
203	
204	def grep_dynamic_imports() -> set[str]:
205	    """Secondary pass: look for importlib.import_module('x'), __import__('x')
206	    calls that static analysis misses."""
207	    dynamic: set[str] = set()
208	    for py in list(SRC.rglob("*.py")) + list(SCRIPTS.rglob("*.py")):
209	        if "__pycache__" in py.parts:
210	            continue
211	        try:
212	            text = py.read_text(encoding="utf-8", errors="replace")
213	        except OSError:
214	            continue
215	        # Quick-and-dirty patterns
216	        import re
217	        for m in re.finditer(r"""importlib\.import_module\(['"]([^'"]+)['"]""", text):
218	            dynamic.add(m.group(1))
219	        for m in re.finditer(r"""__import__\(['"]([^'"]+)['"]""", text):
220	            dynamic.add(m.group(1))
221	    return dynamic
222	
223	
224	def main() -> int:
225	    print(f"Scanning {SRC} and {SCRIPTS}...", file=sys.stderr)
226	    all_files = scan_all_python_files(SRC) | scan_all_python_files(SCRIPTS)
227	    print(f"  total .py files: {len(all_files)}", file=sys.stderr)
228	
229	    module_index = build_module_index(all_files)
230	
231	    live = compute_live_closure(ENTRY_POINTS, all_files, module_index)
232	    print(f"  live (reachable from entry points): {len(live)}", file=sys.stderr)
233	
234	    dead = sorted(set(all_files) - live, key=lambda p: str(p))
235	    print(f"  orphaned (NOT reachable): {len(dead)}", file=sys.stderr)
236	
237	    dynamic = grep_dynamic_imports()
238	    print(f"  dynamic imports detected: {len(dynamic)}", file=sys.stderr)
239	
240	    # Group orphans properly:
241	    #   src/<subpkg>/... -> group key = "src/<subpkg>"
242	    #   scripts/...      -> group key = "scripts"
243	    groups: dict[str, list[Path]] = {}
244	    for p in dead:
245	        try:
246	            rel = p.relative_to(ROOT)
247	        except ValueError:
248	            rel = p
249	        parts = rel.parts
250	        if parts[0] == "scripts":
251	            key = "scripts"
252	        elif parts[0] == "src" and len(parts) >= 2:
253	            key = f"src/{parts[1]}"
254	        else:
255	            key = parts[0]
256	        groups.setdefault(key, []).append(p)
257	
258	    # Write report
259	    out_md = ROOT / "docs" / "live_code_audit.md"
260	    out_md.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
261	    lines: list[str] = []
262	    lines.append("# Live-code audit — 2026-04-18")
263	    lines.append("")
264	    lines.append(
265	        f"Static import-closure analysis from {len(ENTRY_POINTS)} entry points "
266	        f"(orchestrators + preflight + tests). Produced by "
267	        f"`scripts/audit_live_code.py`."
268	    )
269	    lines.append("")
270	    lines.append(f"- Total `.py` files under `src/` + `scripts/`: **{len(all_files)}**")
271	    lines.append(f"- Reachable from entry points (LIVE): **{len(live)}**")
272	    lines.append(f"- Not reachable (ORPHAN candidates): **{len(dead)}**")
273	    lines.append(f"- Dynamic imports detected: **{len(dynamic)}** "
274	                 f"(listed in appendix — may make orphans live)")
275	    lines.append("")
276	    lines.append("## Orphan candidates by subpackage")
277	    lines.append("")
278	    lines.append(
279	        "Files below are NOT reachable from any entry point via static "
280	        "import analysis. They may still be used via dynamic imports "
281	        "(see appendix) or as standalone scripts. Archive only after "
282	        "human review."
283	    )
284	    lines.append("")
285	    for top in sorted(groups.keys()):
286	        lines.append(f"### `{top}/` — {len(groups[top])} orphan file(s)")
287	        lines.append("")
288	        lines.append("| File | Size | Last commit | First docstring line |")
289	        lines.append("|------|------|-------------|----------------------|")
290	        for p in groups[top]:
291	            try:
292	                rel = p.relative_to(ROOT)
293	            except ValueError:
294	                rel = p
295	            size_kb = p.stat().st_size // 1024
296	            date = git_last_commit_date(p)
297	            doc = first_docstring(p).replace("|", "\\|")
298	            lines.append(f"| `{rel.as_posix()}` | {size_kb}K | {date} | {doc} |")
299	        lines.append("")
300	    lines.append("## Entry points used")
301	    lines.append("")
302	    for ep in ENTRY_POINTS[:10]:
303	        try:
304	            rel = ep.relative_to(ROOT)
305	        except ValueError:
306	            rel = ep
307	        lines.append(f"- `{rel.as_posix()}`")
308	    if len(ENTRY_POINTS) > 10:
309	        lines.append(f"- ... plus {len(ENTRY_POINTS) - 10} test files under `tests/`")
310	    lines.append("")
311	    lines.append("## Dynamic imports appendix")
312	    lines.append("")
313	    if dynamic:
314	        for d in sorted(dynamic):
315	            lines.append(f"- `{d}`")
316	    else:
317	        lines.append("_None detected._")
318	    out_md.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
319	    print(f"Report written: {out_md}", file=sys.stderr)
320	
321	    # Also write a machine-readable summary
322	    out_json = ROOT / "docs" / "live_code_audit.json"
323	    out_json.write_text(json.dumps({
324	        "total_files": len(all_files),
325	        "live_count": len(live),
326	        "orphan_count": len(dead),
327	        "orphans_by_top": {k: [str(p.relative_to(ROOT)) for p in v]
328	                           for k, v in groups.items()},
329	        "dynamic_imports": sorted(dynamic),
330	    }, indent=2), encoding="utf-8")
331	    return 0
332	
333	
334	if __name__ == "__main__":
335	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""Forensic audit script for POLARIS v3 Hybrid reports.
2	
3	Measures analytical depth, structural quality, and surface metrics
4	across all 4 quality layers. Designed for head-to-head comparison
5	against v1 (PG_TEST_039) and v2 (V2_E2E_007) baselines.
6	
7	Usage:
8	    python scripts/audit_v3_report.py <report_md_path> [--json]
9	"""
10	
11	import argparse
12	import json
13	import re
14	import sys
15	from collections import Counter
16	from pathlib import Path
17	
18	
19	def audit_report(report_md: str) -> dict:
20	    """Full forensic audit of a v3 report.
21	
22	    Checks 4 quality layers:
23	    1. Core Analytical Depth (comparison, aggregation, challenge)
24	    2. Structure (duplicates, cross-references, coherence)
25	    3. Integrity (citations, filler, banned patterns)
26	    4. Surface (tables, key findings, word count)
27	
28	    Args:
29	        report_md: Full markdown text of the report.
30	
31	    Returns:
32	        Dict of audit metrics.
33	    """
34	    # --- Layer 1: Core Analytical Depth ---
35	    comparison_markers = len(re.findall(
36	        r'\b(compared to|in contrast|whereas|however|unlike|alternatively|'
37	        r'on the other hand|differs from|outperformed|underperformed)\b',
38	        report_md, re.I,
39	    ))
40	    aggregation_patterns = len(re.findall(
41	        r'\b(across \d+ studies|multiple sources|ranged from|median of|'
42	        r'average of|converging|majority of evidence|consistently)\b',
43	        report_md, re.I,
44	    ))
45	    challenge_markers = len(re.findall(
46	        r'\b(limitation|conflicting|gap in|remains unclear|caveat|'
47	        r'insufficient evidence|notable absence|further research needed)\b',
48	        report_md, re.I,
49	    ))
50	    explanation_markers = len(re.findall(
51	        r'\b(because|due to|mechanism|attributed to|driven by|'
52	        r'resulting from|implication|significance)\b',
53	        report_md, re.I,
54	    ))
55	
56	    # --- Layer 2: Structure ---
57	    sentences = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+', report_md)
58	    non_trivial = [s.strip().lower() for s in sentences if len(s.strip()) > 30]
59	    unique_sentences = set(non_trivial)
60	    dup_ratio = 1 - len(unique_sentences) / max(len(non_trivial), 1)
61	
62	    cross_refs = len(re.findall(
63	        r'as discussed in \[', report_md, re.I,
64	    ))
65	    # Section count
66	    sections = re.findall(r'^## .+', report_md, re.M)
67	    section_count = len(sections)
68	
69	    # --- Layer 3: Integrity ---
70	    citations = re.findall(r'\[\d+\]', report_md)
71	    total_citations = len(citations)
72	    unique_citation_numbers = len(set(citations))
73	    words = report_md.split()
74	    word_count = len(words)
75	    cite_density = total_citations / max(word_count, 1) * 100
76	
77	    # Filler detection
78	    filler_phrases = re.findall(
79	        r'\b(furthermore|it is important to note|interestingly|'
80	        r'in conclusion|it should be noted that|notably|'
81	        r'it is worth mentioning)\b',
82	        report_md, re.I,
83	    )
84	    filler_count = len(filler_phrases)
85	
86	    # Sequential source pattern (bad: "Study A... Study B... Study C...")
87	    sequential_summaries = len(re.findall(
88	        r'(?:study|research|investigation|analysis)\s+\w+\s+(?:found|showed|reported|demonstrated)',
89	        report_md, re.I,
90	    ))
91	
92	    # Banned patterns
93	    garbled_text = len(re.findall(r'â€|Ã[¡-¼]|\ufffd', report_md))
94	
95	    # --- Layer 4: Surface ---
96	    table_rows = re.findall(r'\|[^|]+\|[^|]+\|', report_md)
97	    # Exclude header separator rows (|---|---|)
98	    data_table_rows = [
99	        r for r in table_rows
100	        if not re.match(r'\|\s*[-:]+\s*\|', r)
101	    ]
102	    table_count = 0
103	    in_table = False
104	    for line in report_md.split('\n'):
105	        if re.match(r'\|.+\|.+\|', line.strip()):
106	            if not in_table:
107	                table_count += 1
108	                in_table = True
109	        else:
110	            in_table = False
111	    # Approximate: each table has header + separator + data rows
112	    table_count = max(table_count // 3, table_count > 0)
113	
114	    # Simpler: count distinct table blocks (header rows followed by separator)
115	    table_blocks = len(re.findall(
116	        r'\|[^|\n]+\|[^|\n]+\|\n\|[\s:|-]+\|',
117	        report_md,
118	    ))
119	
120	    key_findings = len(re.findall(
121	        r'\*\*Key Findings?\*\*', report_md, re.I,
122	    ))
123	
124	    # Bibliography size
125	    bib_entries = len(re.findall(r'^\[\d+\]', report_md, re.M))
126	
127	    return {
128	        # Layer 1: Analytical Depth
129	        "comparison_markers": comparison_markers,
130	        "aggregation_patterns": aggregation_patterns,
131	        "challenge_markers": challenge_markers,
132	        "explanation_markers": explanation_markers,
133	        # Layer 2: Structure
134	        "word_count": word_count,
135	        "section_count": section_count,
136	        "dup_ratio_pct": round(dup_ratio * 100, 1),
137	        "cross_section_refs": cross_refs,
138	        # Layer 3: Integrity
139	        "total_citations": total_citations,
140	        "unique_citation_numbers": unique_citation_numbers,
141	        "citation_density_per_100w": round(cite_density, 2),
142	        "filler_count": filler_count,
143	        "sequential_summaries": sequential_summaries,
144	        "garbled_text_instances": garbled_text,
145	        # Layer 4: Surface
146	        "table_blocks": table_blocks,
147	        "key_findings_sections": key_findings,
148	        "bibliography_entries": bib_entries,
149	    }
150	
151	
152	def _grade(metrics: dict) -> str:
153	    """Assign a letter grade based on audit metrics."""
154	    score = 0
155	
156	    # Analytical depth (40 points)
157	    score += min(metrics["comparison_markers"], 20)
158	    score += min(metrics["aggregation_patterns"], 10)
159	    score += min(metrics["challenge_markers"], 5)
160	    score += min(metrics["explanation_markers"], 5)
161	
162	    # Structure (20 points)
163	    if metrics["dup_ratio_pct"] < 1:
164	        score += 10
165	    elif metrics["dup_ratio_pct"] < 5:
166	        score += 5
167	    score += min(metrics["cross_section_refs"], 5)
168	    score += min(metrics["key_findings_sections"], 5)
169	
170	    # Integrity (25 points)
171	    if metrics["citation_density_per_100w"] >= 2.0:
172	        score += 10
173	    elif metrics["citation_density_per_100w"] >= 1.0:
174	        score += 5
175	    score += max(0, 5 - metrics["filler_count"])
176	    score += max(0, 5 - metrics["garbled_text_instances"])
177	    if metrics["bibliography_entries"] >= 15:
178	        score += 5
179	    elif metrics["bibliography_entries"] >= 8:
180	        score += 3
181	
182	    # Surface (15 points)
183	    score += min(metrics["table_blocks"] * 3, 9)
184	    if metrics["word_count"] >= 8000:
185	        score += 3
186	    elif metrics["word_count"] >= 5000:
187	        score += 2
188	    if metrics["section_count"] >= 6:
189	        score += 3
190	
191	    if score >= 85:
192	        return "A+"
193	    elif score >= 75:
194	        return "A"
195	    elif score >= 65:
196	        return "B+"
197	    elif score >= 55:
198	        return "B"
199	    elif score >= 45:
200	        return "B-"
201	    elif score >= 35:
202	        return "C+"
203	    elif score >= 25:
204	        return "C"
205	    return "D"
206	
207	
208	def main():
209	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="POLARIS v3 Report Forensic Audit")
210	    parser.add_argument("report_path", help="Path to the report markdown file")
211	    parser.add_argument("--json", action="store_true", help="Output as JSON")
212	    args = parser.parse_args()
213	
214	    path = Path(args.report_path)
215	    if not path.exists():
216	        print(f"Error: {path} not found", file=sys.stderr)
217	        sys.exit(1)
218	
219	    report_text = path.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
220	    metrics = audit_report(report_text)
221	    grade = _grade(metrics)
222	    metrics["grade"] = grade
223	
224	    if args.json:
225	        print(json.dumps(metrics, indent=2))
226	    else:
227	        print(f"{'='*60}")
228	        print(f"POLARIS v3 Forensic Audit: {path.name}")
229	        print(f"{'='*60}")
230	        print()
231	        print(f"  Grade: {grade}")
232	        print(f"  Words: {metrics['word_count']:,}")
233	        print(f"  Sections: {metrics['section_count']}")
234	        print()
235	        print("  Layer 1: Analytical Depth")
236	        print(f"    Comparison markers:  {metrics['comparison_markers']:>4}")
237	        print(f"    Aggregation patterns:{metrics['aggregation_patterns']:>4}")
238	        print(f"    Challenge markers:   {metrics['challenge_markers']:>4}")
239	        print(f"    Explanation markers: {metrics['explanation_markers']:>4}")
240	        print()
241	        print("  Layer 2: Structure")
242	        print(f"    Duplicate ratio:     {metrics['dup_ratio_pct']:>5.1f}%")
243	        print(f"    Cross-section refs:  {metrics['cross_section_refs']:>4}")
244	        print()
245	        print("  Layer 3: Integrity")
246	        print(f"    Citations:           {metrics['total_citations']:>4} ({metrics['unique_citation_numbers']} unique)")
247	        print(f"    Citation density:    {metrics['citation_density_per_100w']:>5.2f} per 100 words")
248	        print(f"    Filler phrases:      {metrics['filler_count']:>4}")
249	        print(f"    Sequential summaries:{metrics['sequential_summaries']:>4}")
250	        print(f"    Garbled text:        {metrics['garbled_text_instances']:>4}")
251	        print()
252	        print("  Layer 4: Surface")
253	        print(f"    Tables:              {metrics['table_blocks']:>4}")
254	        print(f"    Key Findings:        {metrics['key_findings_sections']:>4}")
255	        print(f"    Bibliography:        {metrics['bibliography_entries']:>4}")
256	        print(f"{'='*60}")
257	
258	
259	if __name__ == "__main__":
260	    main()

1	"""Shadow harness — run ON vs OFF over the clinical S2 fixture (GH #983).
2	
3	Emits a per-URL diff + a directional confusion matrix so a human reviewer can
4	see exactly where the field-agnostic authority view diverges from the legacy
5	clinical tier. Offline; no network. Read-only — does not gate anything.
6	
7	Usage:
8	    python scripts/authority_shadow_diff.py
9	"""
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import collections
13	import json
14	import os
15	import sys
16	from pathlib import Path
17	
18	REPO_ROOT = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1]
19	if str(REPO_ROOT) not in sys.path:
20	    sys.path.insert(0, str(REPO_ROOT))
21	
22	FIXTURE = REPO_ROOT / "tests" / "fixtures" / "authority" / "clinical_200_urls.jsonl"
23	
24	
25	def main() -> None:
26	    rows = [
27	        json.loads(ln)
28	        for ln in FIXTURE.read_text(encoding="utf-8").splitlines()
29	        if ln.strip()
30	    ]
31	
32	    os.environ["PG_USE_AUTHORITY_MODEL"] = "1"
33	    from src.polaris_graph.authority import AuthoritySignals
34	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.tier_classifier import (
35	        ClassificationSignals,
36	        classify_source_tier,
37	    )
38	
39	    matches = 0
40	    confusion: dict[str, int] = collections.Counter()
41	    diffs: list[dict] = []
42	    for e in rows:
43	        s = e["signals"]
44	        sig = ClassificationSignals(
45	            url=e["url"],
46	            title=e["title"],
47	            fetched_content_length=s["fetched_content_length"],
48	            openalex_publication_type=s["openalex_publication_type"],
49	            openalex_source_type=s["openalex_source_type"],
50	            openalex_is_peer_reviewed=s["openalex_is_peer_reviewed"],
51	        )
52	        sig.authority = AuthoritySignals(**e["authority_signals"])
53	        result = classify_source_tier(sig)
54	        view = result.tier.value
55	        head = e["head_tier"]
56	        confusion[f"{head}->{view}"] += 1
57	        if view == head:
58	            matches += 1
59	        else:
60	            diffs.append({
61	                "url": e["url"],
62	                "head_tier": head,
63	                "view_tier": view,
64	                "head_rule": e["head_rule"],
65	                "source_class": result.source_class,
66	                "authority_score": result.authority_score,
67	                "authority_confidence": result.authority_confidence,
68	            })
69	
70	    print(f"shadow diff over {len(rows)} clinical URLs")
71	    print(f"agreement: {matches}/{len(rows)} = {matches / len(rows):.3f}")
72	    print("confusion (head->view : count):")
73	    for k, v in sorted(confusion.items()):
74	        print(f"  {k} : {v}")
75	    print(f"\n{len(diffs)} per-URL diffs:")
76	    for d in diffs:
77	        print(
78	            f"  {d['head_tier']}->{d['view_tier']} [{d['head_rule']}] "
79	            f"class={d['source_class']} conf={d['authority_confidence']} {d['url'][:60]}"
80	        )
81	
82

223	def _canonical_serialize(verdict: dict) -> bytes:
224	    payload = {k: v for k, v in verdict.items() if k != "hmac_sha256"}
225	    return json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")

228	def _audit_log_event(event: dict) -> None:
229	    try:
230	        AUDIT_LOG.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
231	        prev_sha = None
232	        if AUDIT_LOG.exists():
233	            with open(AUDIT_LOG, "rb") as f:
234	                f.seek(0, 2)
235	                size = f.tell()
236	                f.seek(max(0, size - 4096))
237	                tail = f.read().decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
238	            for line in reversed(tail.strip().splitlines()):
239	                try:
240	                    last = json.loads(line)
241	                    prev_sha = last.get("entry_sha")
242	                    break
243	                except Exception:
244	                    continue
245	        event["prev_sha"] = prev_sha
246	        payload = {k: v for k, v in event.items() if k != "entry_sha"}
247	        ser = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
248	        event["entry_sha"] = hashlib.sha256(ser).hexdigest()
249	        with open(AUDIT_LOG, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
250	            f.write(json.dumps(event, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
251	    except Exception:
252	        pass

255	def main() -> int:
256	    hmac_key = HMAC_KEY_PATH.read_bytes().strip()
257	    pin_sha = hashlib.sha256(CANONICAL_PIN.read_bytes()).hexdigest()
258	    timestamp = time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime())
259	    backfill_session_id = f"pre_bootstrap_backfill_{int(time.time())}"
260	
261	    written = []
262	    skipped = []
263	    for task_id, info in PRE_BOOTSTRAP_COMPLETED.items():
264	        # Verify evidence files exist
265	        missing = [p for p in info["evidence"] if not (POLARIS_ROOT / p).exists()]
266	        if missing:
267	            skipped.append((task_id, f"missing evidence: {missing}"))
268	            continue
269	
270	        verdict_path = VERDICT_DIR / task_id / "iter_1.json"
271	        if verdict_path.exists():
272	            skipped.append((task_id, "verdict already exists"))
273	            continue
274	
275	        verdict = {
276	            "schema_version": "1.0.0",
277	            "task_id": task_id,
278	            "iter": 1,
279	            "codex_session_id": backfill_session_id,
280	            "model": "gpt-5.5",  # nominal — this verdict is human-attested, not Codex-generated
281	            "reasoning_effort": "xhigh",
282	            "canonical_pin_sha": pin_sha,
283	            "commit_sha": "0" * 40,  # backfill — pre-dates §C gate
284	            "diff_sha256": hashlib.sha256(b"pre_bootstrap_backfill").hexdigest(),
285	            "verdict": "APPROVE",
286	            "verdict_reason": (
287	                f"Pre-bootstrap backfill for task {task_id} ({info['title']}). "
288	                f"Task completed BEFORE Plan v13 §C gate became operational. "
289	                f"This verdict is HUMAN-ATTESTED via the substrate evidence listed in "
290	                f"manifest_evidence; it is NOT a Codex-generated verdict and does not "
291	                f"satisfy the §C-server CI re-validation requirement. The §C-server CI "
292	                f"gate will not fire on these tasks because no commit triggers it; "
293	                f"these tasks pre-date the gate. Future commits to these task scopes "
294	                f"WILL be gated normally."
295	            ),
296	            "findings": [],
297	            "manifest_evidence": info["evidence"],
298	            "rationale": info["rationale"],
299	            "timestamp": timestamp,
300	            "hmac_sha256": "",  # filled below
301	        }
302	        # Compute HMAC over canonical payload (excluding hmac_sha256 itself)
303	        verdict["hmac_sha256"] = hmac.new(hmac_key, _canonical_serialize(verdict), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
304	
305	        verdict_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
306	        verdict_path.write_text(json.dumps(verdict, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
307	        written.append(task_id)
308	
309	        _audit_log_event({
310	            "ts": timestamp,
311	            "type": "pre_bootstrap_backfill",
312	            "task_id": task_id,
313	            "verdict_path": str(verdict_path.relative_to(POLARIS_ROOT)),
314	            "evidence": info["evidence"],
315	            "session_id": backfill_session_id,
316	        })

318	    print(f"backfill: wrote {len(written)} verdicts -> {written}")
319	    if skipped:

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""POLARIS secret-pattern scanner — Plan v13 §C-scan.
3	
4	Scans verdict-rerun output (and proxy logs) for credential exfiltration
5	patterns. Used by GitHub Actions verdict-validate job; runs on scrubbed
6	output BEFORE artifact publish.
7	
8	Per Codex round-9 review: covers documented exfil targets including
9	OpenAI/Anthropic/GitHub/AWS/GCP/Slack/Stripe/PEM/Bearer-auth/basic-auth.
10	"""
11	
12	from __future__ import annotations
13	
14	import argparse
15	import json
16	import re
17	import sys
18	from pathlib import Path
19	
20	# Compiled patterns. Each is (regex, friendly_name).
21	SECRET_PATTERNS: list[tuple[re.Pattern, str]] = [
22	    # OpenAI (sk-, sk-admin-, sk-proj-, sk-svcacct-)
23	    (re.compile(r"sk-(?:admin|proj|svcacct)?-?[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}"), "openai_api_key"),
24	    # Anthropic
25	    (re.compile(r"sk-ant-(?:api|admin)\d+-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}"), "anthropic_api_key"),
26	    # GitHub PATs / tokens
27	    (re.compile(r"gh[pousr]_[A-Za-z0-9_]{36,}"), "github_token"),
28	    (re.compile(r"github_pat_[A-Za-z0-9_]{20,}"), "github_pat"),
29	    # AWS access key IDs (long-lived + temporary)
30	    (re.compile(r"\b(?:AKIA|ASIA)[A-Z0-9]{16}\b"), "aws_key_id"),
31	    # GitLab
32	    (re.compile(r"glpat-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}"), "gitlab_pat"),
33	    # Hugging Face
34	    (re.compile(r"hf_[A-Za-z0-9]{30,}"), "huggingface_token"),
35	    # NPM
36	    (re.compile(r"npm_[A-Za-z0-9]{30,}"), "npm_token"),
37	    # PyPI
38	    (re.compile(r"pypi-[A-Za-z0-9_-]{30,}"), "pypi_token"),
39	    # Slack
40	    (re.compile(r"xox[baprs]-[A-Za-z0-9-]{20,}"), "slack_token"),
41	    # Google
42	    (re.compile(r"AIza[A-Za-z0-9_-]{30,}"), "google_api_key"),
43	    (re.compile(r"ya29\.[A-Za-z0-9_-]{20,}"), "google_oauth"),
44	    # Stripe
45	    (re.compile(r"sk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}"), "stripe_secret"),
46	    (re.compile(r"rk_live_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}"), "stripe_restricted"),
47	    # PEM blocks (covers RSA, EC, OPENSSH, DSA, PGP)
48	    (re.compile(r"-----BEGIN [A-Z ]*PRIVATE KEY-----"), "pem_private_key"),
49	    (re.compile(r"-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----"), "openssh_private_key"),
50	    # Generic bearer / basic auth
51	    (re.compile(r"Authorization:\s*Bearer\s+[A-Za-z0-9._\-]{20,}", re.IGNORECASE), "bearer_token"),
52	    (re.compile(r"https?://[^:\s]+:[^@\s]+@"), "basic_auth_url"),
53	    # I-sec-001 (#535): vendor keys missed by the original list — these are
54	    # exactly the shapes that leaked into committed .codex/ transcripts and
55	    # were not caught by GitHub push protection either.
56	    (re.compile(r"jina_[A-Za-z0-9_]{20,}"), "jina_api_key"),
57	    (re.compile(r"\bfc-[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}\b"), "firecrawl_api_key"),
58	    (re.compile(r"\bfw_[A-Za-z0-9]{20,}"), "fireworks_api_key"),
59	    # Configured POLARIS .env secret-var NAME followed by a key-shaped value —
60	    # a vendor-agnostic catch for a `.env` dump (Exa / Semantic-Scholar / Vast
61	    # and any future credential have no distinctive value prefix).
62	    (re.compile(
63	        r"(?i)\b(?:JINA_API_KEY|EXA_API_KEY|FIRECRAWL_API_KEY|FIREWORKS_API_KEY"
64	        r"|GEMINI_API_KEY|SEMANTIC_SCHOLAR_API_KEY|VAST_API_KEY"
65	        r"|OPENROUTER_API_KEY|OPEN_PAGERANK_API_KEY|NCBI_API_KEY"
66	        r"|OVH_APPLICATION_KEY|OVH_APPLICATION_SECRET|OVH_CONSUMER_KEY"
67	        r"|POLARIS_AUTH_SECRET|POLARIS_JWT_SECRET)"
68	        r"""\s*[:=]\s*["']?[A-Za-z0-9._/+~=-]{20,}"""),
69	     "configured_secret_assignment"),
70	]
71	
72	
73	def scan(path: Path) -> list[dict]:
74	    text = path.read_text(errors="replace")
75	    hits = []
76	    for pattern, name in SECRET_PATTERNS:
77	        for match in pattern.finditer(text):
78	            value = match.group(0)
79	            hits.append({
80	                "pattern": name,
81	                "match_prefix": value[:8] + "...(redacted)",
82	                "match_length": len(value),
83	                "file": str(path),
84	                "offset": match.start(),
85	            })
86	    return hits
87	
88	
89	def main() -> int:
90	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
91	    parser.add_argument("paths", nargs="+", help="Files to scan")
92	    parser.add_argument("--strict", action="store_true",
93	                       help="Exit 1 on any match (default: exit 0 with stderr report)")
94	    args = parser.parse_args()
95	
96	    all_hits = []
97	    for p in args.paths:
98	        path = Path(p)
99	        if not path.exists():
100	            print(f"scan_for_secrets: WARN file missing {p}", file=sys.stderr)
101	            continue
102	        all_hits.extend(scan(path))
103	
104	    if all_hits:
105	        print(json.dumps({"secrets_detected": all_hits}, indent=2), file=sys.stderr)
106	        if args.strict:
107	            return 1
108	    return 0
109	
110	
111	if __name__ == "__main__":
112	    sys.exit(main())

137	def _verify_canonical_pin() -> tuple[bool, str]:
138	    pin_text = _git_show_head("docs/canonical_pin.txt")
139	    if pin_text is None:
140	        return False, "canonical_pin.txt missing from HEAD"
141	    expected = {}
142	    for line in pin_text.splitlines():
143	        line = line.strip()
144	        if not line or line.startswith("#"):
145	            continue
146	        parts = line.split(None, 1)
147	        if len(parts) == 2:
148	            expected[parts[1]] = parts[0]
149	    for f in CANONICAL_FILES:
150	        head_content = _git_show_head(f)
151	        if head_content is None:
152	            return False, f"{f} missing from HEAD"
153	        head_sha = hashlib.sha256(head_content.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
154	        if expected.get(f) != head_sha:
155	            return False, f"HEAD/pin mismatch for {f}"
156	        wt = POLARIS_ROOT / f
157	        if not wt.exists():
158	            return False, f"{f} missing in working tree"
159	        if _file_sha256(wt) != head_sha:
160	            return False, f"working-tree drift for {f}"
161	    return True, "canonical pin verified"

208	def _next_actionable(matrix: dict) -> tuple[str | None, dict | None, str | None]:
209	    """Walk plan-canonical sequence; return (task_id, task_dict, prep_id_or_None)."""
210	    for phase_key in sorted(matrix.keys()):
211	        if not phase_key.startswith("phase_"):
212	            continue
213	        phase = matrix[phase_key]
214	        if not isinstance(phase, dict):
215	            continue
216	        for task_key, task_val in phase.items():
217	            if not isinstance(task_val, dict):
218	                continue
219	            task_id = task_val.get("task_id") or task_key.removeprefix("task_").replace("_", ".")
220	            verdict, _ = _verdict_state(task_id)
221	            if verdict == "APPROVE":
222	                continue
223	            # Skip tasks with active halt-resolution markers (e.g. 5.2 deferred to Phase 5)
224	            if _is_halt_resolved(task_id):
225	                continue
226	            if task_val.get("user_action"):
227	                # Check substrate_prep
228	                preps = task_val.get("substrate_prep", []) or []
229	                for prep in preps:
230	                    if not isinstance(prep, dict):
231	                        continue
232	                    prep_id = prep.get("id")
233	                    if not prep_id:
234	                        continue
235	                    if _verdict_state(prep_id)[0] == "APPROVE":
236	                        continue
237	                    if _is_halt_resolved(prep_id):
238	                        continue
239	                    return task_id, task_val, prep_id
240	                # User-action with no pending prep → skip
241	                continue
242	            return task_id, task_val, None
243	    return None, None, None

528	    verdict.setdefault("schema_version", "1.0.0")
529	    verdict["task_id"] = task_id
530	    verdict["iter"] = iter_n
531	    verdict["canonical_pin_sha"] = hashlib.sha256(CANONICAL_PIN.read_bytes()).hexdigest()
532	    verdict["diff_sha256"] = diff_sha
533	    verdict.setdefault("commit_sha", "0" * 40)  # gate ignores this for backfill+normal
534	    verdict.setdefault("model", "gpt-5.5")
535	    verdict.setdefault("reasoning_effort", "xhigh")
536	    verdict.setdefault("findings", [])
537	    verdict.setdefault("timestamp", time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime()))
538	
539	    # HMAC sign
540	    hmac_key = HMAC_KEY_PATH.read_bytes().strip()
541	    payload = {k: v for k, v in verdict.items() if k != "hmac_sha256"}
542	    ser = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
543	    verdict["hmac_sha256"] = hmac.new(hmac_key, ser, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
544	    out_path.write_text(json.dumps(verdict, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
545	
546	    return verdict

549	def _commit_substrate_with_verdict(task_id: str, iter_n: int, verdict_path: Path) -> str:
550	    """P1-2 fix: orchestrator stages substrate + verdict atomically + commits.
551	
552	    Called only after Codex APPROVE. Returns commit SHA.
553	    """
554	    # Stage all working-tree changes, including the just-written verdict
555	    subprocess.run(["git", "add", "-A"], cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), check=True, timeout=30)
556	
557	    pin_short = hashlib.sha256(CANONICAL_PIN.read_bytes()).hexdigest()[:12]
558	    msg = (
559	        f"task {task_id}: iter {iter_n} APPROVE\n\n"
560	        f"task: {task_id}\n"
561	        f"verdict: {verdict_path.relative_to(POLARIS_ROOT).as_posix()}\n"
562	        f"pin: {pin_short}\n"
563	        f"manifest: outputs/audits/manifests/{task_id}.json\n"
564	        f"\n"
565	        f"Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>\n"
566	    )
567	    r = subprocess.run(
568	        ["git", "commit", "-m", msg],
569	        cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=60,
570	    )
571	    if r.returncode != 0:
572	        raise HaltCondition(
573	            7, f"git commit failed (gate may have blocked): {r.stderr[:500]}",
574	            task_id=task_id,
575	        )
576	    sha_proc = subprocess.run(
577	        ["git", "rev-parse", "HEAD"],
578	        cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10,
579	    )

582	
583	def _push_to_origin(task_id: str, iter_n: int) -> None:
584	    """Push to a task-scoped branch and open PR.
585	
586	    Codex round-3 P0 fix: fail-CLOSED on push/PR failure. Was: WARN+continue
587	    (which let task be locally APPROVE'd while CI never ran → server-side
588	    cryptographic gate bypassed). Now: HaltCondition #7 raises on any push/PR
589	    failure that isn't "PR already exists" idempotency.
590	
591	    Per Plan v13 §C-server: orchestrator must NOT push directly to `polaris`.
592	    Branch protection on `polaris` requires PR-only via merge queue.
593	    """
594	    branch = f"task/{task_id}/iter_{iter_n}"
595	    subprocess.run(
596	        ["git", "branch", "-f", branch, "HEAD"],
597	        cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), check=True, timeout=10,
598	    )
599	    r = subprocess.run(
600	        ["git", "push", "-f", "origin", branch],
601	        cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120,
602	    )
603	    if r.returncode != 0:
604	        raise HaltCondition(
605	            7, f"git push of {branch} failed (server-side gate cannot run): {r.stderr[:300]}",
606	            task_id=task_id,
607	        )
608	    pr_title = f"task {task_id}: iter {iter_n} APPROVE"
609	    pr_body = (
610	        f"Per Plan v13 autoloop. Task {task_id} iteration {iter_n}.\n\n"
611	        f"Codex local verdict: APPROVE (HMAC-signed).\n"
612	        f"CI Phase B/C will independently re-validate.\n\n"
613	        f"Auto-merge enabled if CI passes.\n"
614	    )
615	    pr_r = subprocess.run(
616	        ["gh", "pr", "create", "--base", "polaris", "--head", branch,
617	         "--title", pr_title, "--body", pr_body],
618	        cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30,
619	    )
620	    if pr_r.returncode != 0:
621	        if "already exists" in (pr_r.stderr or "") or "already exists" in (pr_r.stdout or ""):
622	            print(f"INFO PR for {branch} already exists; reusing", file=sys.stderr)
623	            return
624	        # Any other failure → halt (CI gate cannot run without PR)
625	        raise HaltCondition(
626	            7, f"gh pr create for {branch} failed (CI gate cannot run): {pr_r.stderr[:300]}",
627	            task_id=task_id,
628	        )
629	
630	
631	# ---------- Main loop ----------
632	
633	async def _run_task(task_id: str, task_def: dict) -> None:
634	    deadline = time.time() + PER_TASK_HOURS * 3600
635	    iter_n = 0
636	
637	    while iter_n < MAX_REQUEST_CHANGES_ITER:
638	        iter_n += 1
639	        _heartbeat({"current_task": task_id, "iter": iter_n, "phase": "build"})
640	
641	        # Step 1: build via fresh SDK session (Claude SDK stages files but the
642	        # precommit gate blocks any commit attempt without a verdict; SDK is
643	        # instructed in the build brief NOT to commit — orchestrator does that)
644	        build_brief = _build_task_brief(task_id, task_def)
645	        build_brief_path = BRIEF_DIR / f"{task_id}_build_brief.md"
646	        build_brief_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
647	        build_brief_path.write_text(build_brief, encoding="utf-8")
648	
649	        await _run_agent_session(task_id, build_brief, deadline)
650	
651	        # Step 2: invoke Codex for INDEPENDENT review (not the build brief)
652	        _heartbeat({"current_task": task_id, "iter": iter_n, "phase": "review"})
653	        verdict = _invoke_codex_review(task_id, build_brief_path, iter_n, task_def)
654	        verdict_path = VERDICT_DIR / task_id / f"iter_{iter_n}.json"
655	
656	        if verdict.get("verdict") == "APPROVE":
657	            # Step 3: commit substrate + verdict atomically (P1-2 fix)
658	            _heartbeat({"current_task": task_id, "iter": iter_n, "phase": "commit"})
659	            commit_sha = _commit_substrate_with_verdict(task_id, iter_n, verdict_path)
660	            # Step 4: push to task-scoped branch + open PR (Codex round-2 P0 fix:
661	            # orchestrator MUST NOT push directly to polaris; branch protection
662	            # forces PR-only flow through CI Phase A/B/C verdict-validate)
663	            _heartbeat({"current_task": task_id, "iter": iter_n, "phase": "push", "commit": commit_sha})
664	            _push_to_origin(task_id, iter_n)
665	            _heartbeat({"current_task": task_id, "iter": iter_n, "phase": "approved", "commit": commit_sha})
666	            return
667	
668	        # REQUEST_CHANGES path: surface findings to next SDK session
669	        if iter_n >= MAX_REQUEST_CHANGES_ITER:
670	            raise HaltCondition(
671	                4, f"3 consecutive REQUEST_CHANGES on {task_id}", task_id=task_id,
672	                payload={"latest_verdict": verdict},
673	            )
674	        # Otherwise loop with findings injected into next build brief
675	        # (SDK session reads outputs/audits/verdicts/<id>/iter_<n-1>.json itself)
676	
677	
678	async def _main() -> int:
679	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
680	    parser.add_argument("--once", action="store_true",
681	                       help="Run one task then exit (for smoke testing)")
682	    args = parser.parse_args()
683	
684	    print(f"POLARIS Autoloop Orchestrator — starting at {POLARIS_ROOT}")
685	    _heartbeat({"phase": "starting"})
686	
687	    # Codex round-4 P0 fix: reconcile unpushed locally-APPROVE'd commits BEFORE
688	    # picking any new task. Otherwise a previous run that committed locally but
689	    # failed at push silently leaves an unpushed verdict; on resume,
690	    # _next_actionable() sees the verdict as APPROVE'd and advances past the
691	    # task, skipping the server-side gate entirely.
692	    try:
693	        unpushed = subprocess.run(
694	            ["git", "log", "origin/polaris..HEAD", "--format=%H %s"],
695	            cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10,
696	        )
697	        if unpushed.returncode == 0 and unpushed.stdout.strip():
698	            for line in unpushed.stdout.strip().splitlines():
699	                # Extract task_id from commit message line "task: <id>"
700	                # via subsequent git show
701	                sha = line.split()[0]
702	                msg = subprocess.run(
703	                    ["git", "show", "--no-patch", "--format=%B", sha],
704	                    cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10,
705	                )
706	                task_match = None
707	                for ml in msg.stdout.splitlines():
708	                    if ml.startswith("task:"):
709	                        task_match = ml.split(":", 1)[1].strip()
710	                        break
711	                if task_match:
712	                    # Find iter from commit message; default to 1
713	                    iter_match = 1
714	                    for ml in msg.stdout.splitlines():
715	                        if ml.startswith("verdict:"):
716	                            # path like outputs/audits/verdicts/<id>/iter_N.json
717	                            import re
718	                            m = re.search(r"iter_(\d+)\.json", ml)
719	                            if m:
720	                                iter_match = int(m.group(1))
721	                            break
722	                    print(f"reconciling unpushed commit {sha[:12]} for task {task_match} iter {iter_match}", file=sys.stderr)
723	                    _heartbeat({"phase": "reconcile_push", "commit": sha, "task": task_match})
724	                    _push_to_origin(task_match, iter_match)
725	    except HaltCondition:
726	        raise  # re-raise so outer except handles it
727	    except Exception as e:
728	        print(f"WARN reconcile-push pre-check failed (non-fatal, will retry next iter): {e}", file=sys.stderr)
729	
730	    while True:
731	        try:
732	            ok, msg = _verify_canonical_pin()
733	            if not ok:
734	                raise HaltCondition(1, f"canonical pin drift: {msg}")
735	
736	            matrix = _load_matrix_from_head()
737	            task_id, task_def, prep_id = _next_actionable(matrix)
738	            if task_id is None:
739	                print("All tasks APPROVE'd. Loop complete.")
740	                _heartbeat({"phase": "complete"})
741	                return 0
742	
743	            target = prep_id or task_id
744	            target_def = task_def
745	            if prep_id:
746	                # Find the prep sub-task definition within parent's substrate_prep list
747	                for p in task_def.get("substrate_prep", []) or []:
748	                    if isinstance(p, dict) and p.get("id") == prep_id:
749	                        target_def = p
750	                        break
751	
752	            print(f"Next actionable: {target} (parent={task_id})")
753	            await _run_task(target, target_def)
754	
755	            if args.once:
756	                return 0
757	
758	            time.sleep(5)
759	
760	        except HaltCondition as halt:
761	            marker = _emit_halt_marker(halt)
762	            print(f"HALT condition #{halt.code}: {halt.reason}", file=sys.stderr)
763	            print(f"Marker: {marker}", file=sys.stderr)
764	            _heartbeat({"phase": "halted", "halt_code": halt.code, "halt_marker": str(marker)})
765	            return halt.code
766	        except KeyboardInterrupt:
767	            print("\nOrchestrator interrupted by user (Ctrl-C). Heartbeat preserved.")
768	            _heartbeat({"phase": "interrupted"})
769	            return 0
770	
771	
772	if __name__ == "__main__":

245	            "Bootstrap smoke synthetic verdict. NOT a Codex-generated review; "

247	            "+ canonical-pin + diff_sha256 validation paths. Real Codex review "
248	            "fires on first non-bootstrap commit (decision #10 runner online)."
249	        ),
250	        "findings": [],
251	        "rationale": (
252	            "Smoke task is a no-op substrate (single test fixture). The smoke "
253	            "validates the gate plumbing, not feature correctness."
254	        ),
255	        "manifest_evidence": [
256	            str(SUBSTRATE_PATH.relative_to(POLARIS_ROOT).as_posix()),
257	            str(MANIFEST_PATH.relative_to(POLARIS_ROOT).as_posix()),
258	        ],
259	        "timestamp": timestamp,
260	    }
261	    # HMAC sign
262	    hmac_key = HMAC_KEY_PATH.read_bytes().strip()
263	    payload = {k: v for k, v in verdict.items() if k != "hmac_sha256"}
264	    ser = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
265	    verdict["hmac_sha256"] = hmac.new(hmac_key, ser, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
266	
267	    VERDICT_PATH.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
268	    VERDICT_PATH.write_text(json.dumps(verdict, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False), encoding="utf-8")
269	
270	
271	def step_5_validate_schema():
272	    """Step 5: validate verdict against schema."""
273	    try:
274	        import jsonschema
275	    except ImportError:
276	        print("    note: jsonschema not installed; skipping schema check")
277	        return
278	    schema = json.loads(SCHEMA_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
279	    verdict = json.loads(VERDICT_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
280	    jsonschema.validate(verdict, schema)
281	
282	
283	def step_6_validate_hmac():
284	    """Step 6: re-compute HMAC and verify match."""
285	    verdict = json.loads(VERDICT_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
286	    expected = verdict["hmac_sha256"]
287	    payload = {k: v for k, v in verdict.items() if k != "hmac_sha256"}
288	    ser = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
289	    hmac_key = HMAC_KEY_PATH.read_bytes().strip()
290	    actual = hmac.new(hmac_key, ser, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
291	    if actual != expected:
292	        raise SmokeError(f"HMAC mismatch: expected {expected[:12]}, computed {actual[:12]}")
293	
294	
295	def step_7_run_gate():
296	    """Step 7: run verdict_gate against current staged set + (still un-staged) verdict."""
297	    # First stage the verdict file too
298	    subprocess.run(
299	        ["git", "add", str(VERDICT_PATH)],

301	    )

309	        )

312	def step_8_check_audit_log():
313	    """Step 8: confirm gate appended an entry to outputs/audits/codex_audit.jsonl."""
314	    audit_log = POLARIS_ROOT / "outputs" / "audits" / "codex_audit.jsonl"
315	    if not audit_log.exists():

317	    last_line = audit_log.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip().splitlines()[-1]
318	    entry = json.loads(last_line)

320	        raise SmokeError(f"latest audit entry type {entry.get('type')!r}, expected gate_passed")
321	
322	
323	def main() -> int:
324	    import argparse
325	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
326	    parser.add_argument("--live", action="store_true",
327	                       help="Invoke real `codex exec` instead of synthesizing the verdict")
328	    args = parser.parse_args()
329	
330	    mode = "LIVE (real Codex)" if args.live else "SMOKE (synthetic verdict)"
331	    print(f"POLARIS Bootstrap Smoke — Plan v13 §K Step 16 — mode: {mode}")
332	    print(f"  task_id: {TASK_ID}")

335

337	    step4_label = ("step 4: LIVE codex exec produces signed verdict"
338	                   if args.live else "step 4: synthesize verdict")
339

342	    results.append(_probe("step 2: write manifest", step_2_manifest))
343	    results.append(_probe("step 3: stage substrate", step_3_stage_substrate))
344	    results.append(_probe(step4_label, step4_fn))
345	    results.append(_probe("step 5: schema validates", step_5_validate_schema))
346	    results.append(_probe("step 6: HMAC validates", step_6_validate_hmac))

348	    results.append(_probe("step 8: audit log entry chained", step_8_check_audit_log))
349

351	    passed = sum(results)

353	    print(f"smoke result: {passed}/{total} probes passed")

356	        return 1

358	    print("Server-side probes (j-n + p-r) require self-hosted runner (decision #10).")

363	    return 0

365

367	    sys.exit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""POLARIS verdict validator — runs in GitHub Actions Phase C (verdict-validate).
3	
4	Per Plan v13 §C severity-aware structural-equivalence rule:
5	  PASS iff ALL:
6	    committed.verdict == "APPROVE"
7	    AND rerun.verdict == "APPROVE"
8	    AND jsonschema_valid(committed) AND jsonschema_valid(rerun)
9	    AND hmac_valid(committed, CODEX_HMAC_KEY)
10	    AND committed.canonical_pin_sha == sha256(canonical_pin.txt @ commit)
11	    AND committed.diff_sha256 matches the actual diff at commit
12	    AND count_findings(rerun, severity in {"P0", "P1"}) == 0
13	    AND no_severity_upgrade(committed.findings, rerun.findings)
14	       # for every (file, line) finding in committed, rerun cannot have higher severity at same location
15	  P2/P3 count drift → audit only, NOT merge-blocking.
16	
17	Loaded from refs/heads/main (trusted ref) — PR cannot tamper with this code.
18	"""
19	
20	from __future__ import annotations
21	
22	import argparse
23	import hashlib
24	import hmac
25	import json
26	import os
27	import re
28	import sys
29	from pathlib import Path
30	
31	
32	SEVERITY_RANK = {"P3": 0, "P2": 1, "P1": 2, "P0": 3}
33	
34	
35	def _validate_schema(verdict: dict, schema_path: Path) -> tuple[bool, str]:
36	    try:
37	        import jsonschema
38	        schema = json.loads(schema_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
39	        jsonschema.validate(verdict, schema)
40	        return True, "schema ok"
41	    except ImportError:
42	        return False, "jsonschema package required in CI runner"
43	    except Exception as e:
44	        return False, f"schema invalid: {e}"
45	
46	
47	def _canonical_serialize(verdict: dict) -> bytes:
48	    payload = {k: v for k, v in verdict.items() if k != "hmac_sha256"}
49	    return json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
50	
51	
52	def _validate_hmac(verdict: dict, key: bytes) -> tuple[bool, str]:
53	    expected = verdict.get("hmac_sha256", "")
54	    actual = hmac.new(key, _canonical_serialize(verdict), hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
55	    if not hmac.compare_digest(actual, expected):
56	        return False, f"hmac mismatch: expected {expected[:12]}, computed {actual[:12]}"
57	    return True, "hmac ok"
58	
59	
60	def _validate_canonical_pin(verdict: dict, pin_path: Path) -> tuple[bool, str]:
61	    expected = verdict.get("canonical_pin_sha", "")
62	    actual = hashlib.sha256(pin_path.read_bytes()).hexdigest()
63	    if actual != expected:
64	        return False, f"canonical_pin mismatch: verdict={expected[:12]}, file={actual[:12]}"
65	    return True, "canonical_pin ok"
66	
67	
68	def _validate_diff_sha(verdict: dict, expected_diff_sha: str) -> tuple[bool, str]:
69	    if expected_diff_sha and verdict.get("diff_sha256") != expected_diff_sha:
70	        return False, (
71	            f"diff_sha256 mismatch: verdict={verdict.get('diff_sha256','')[:12]}, "
72	            f"actual={expected_diff_sha[:12]}"
73	        )
74	    return True, "diff_sha ok"
75	
76	
77	def _no_p0_p1(findings: list[dict]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
78	    blockers = [f for f in findings if f.get("severity") in ("P0", "P1")]
79	    if blockers:
80	        return False, f"rerun has {len(blockers)} P0/P1 finding(s)"
81	    return True, "no P0/P1"
82	
83	
84	def _no_severity_upgrade(committed_findings: list[dict], rerun_findings: list[dict]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
85	    """For each (file, line) PRESENT in committed, rerun cannot have HIGHER severity at same location.
86	
87	    Per Codex round-1 P1-5 fix: only check locations that exist in committed.
88	    New findings at NEW locations are NOT severity upgrades — they're audit-only
89	    drift (Plan v13 §C structural-equivalence rule: "P2/P3 count drift → audit only,
90	    not merge-blocking"). New P0/P1 findings at new locations are caught separately
91	    by `_no_p0_p1` rule.
92	    """
93	    committed_by_loc: dict[tuple[str, int], int] = {}
94	    for f in committed_findings:
95	        key = (f.get("file", ""), f.get("line", 0))
96	        sev = SEVERITY_RANK.get(f.get("severity", "P3"), 0)
97	        if key in committed_by_loc:
98	            committed_by_loc[key] = max(committed_by_loc[key], sev)
99	        else:
100	            committed_by_loc[key] = sev
101	
102	    upgrades = []
103	    for f in rerun_findings:
104	        key = (f.get("file", ""), f.get("line", 0))
105	        if key not in committed_by_loc:
106	            continue  # new location → not an upgrade (drift; audit-only)
107	        rerun_sev = SEVERITY_RANK.get(f.get("severity", "P3"), 0)
108	        committed_sev = committed_by_loc[key]
109	        if rerun_sev > committed_sev:
110	            upgrades.append({
111	                "file": key[0],
112	                "line": key[1],
113	                "committed_severity": [s for s, r in SEVERITY_RANK.items() if r == committed_sev][0],
114	                "rerun_severity": f.get("severity"),
115	                "description": f.get("description", "")[:200],
116	            })
117	
118	    if upgrades:
119	        return False, f"severity upgrade at {len(upgrades)} location(s); first: {upgrades[0]}"
120	    return True, "no severity upgrades"
121	
122	
123	def _latest_committed_verdict(committed_dir: Path) -> dict | None:
124	    if not committed_dir.is_dir():
125	        return None
126	    iters = sorted(committed_dir.glob("iter_*.json"))
127	    if not iters:
128	        return None
129	    return json.loads(iters[-1].read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
130	
131	
132	def main() -> int:
133	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
134	    parser.add_argument("--task-id", required=True)
135	    parser.add_argument("--committed-verdict-dir", required=True, type=Path)
136	    parser.add_argument("--rerun-verdict", required=True, type=Path)
137	    parser.add_argument("--schema", required=True, type=Path)
138	    parser.add_argument("--gpg-public-key", required=True, type=Path)
139	    parser.add_argument("--canonical-pin", required=True, type=Path)
140	    parser.add_argument("--commit-sha", required=True)
141	    parser.add_argument("--diff-sha256", default="")
142	    args = parser.parse_args()
143	
144	    hmac_key_str = os.environ.get("CODEX_HMAC_KEY", "")
145	    if not hmac_key_str:
146	        print("verdict_validator: BLOCK CODEX_HMAC_KEY env not set", file=sys.stderr)
147	        return 1
148	    hmac_key = hmac_key_str.encode() if isinstance(hmac_key_str, str) else hmac_key_str
149	
150	    committed = _latest_committed_verdict(args.committed_verdict_dir)
151	    if committed is None:
152	        print(f"verdict_validator: BLOCK no committed verdict in {args.committed_verdict_dir}", file=sys.stderr)
153	        return 1
154	
155	    try:
156	        rerun = json.loads(args.rerun_verdict.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
157	    except Exception as e:
158	        print(f"verdict_validator: BLOCK rerun verdict unreadable: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
159	        return 1
160	
161	    failures = []
162	
163	    # Codex round-3 P0 fix: verdict.task_id MUST match --task-id arg from workflow.
164	    # Was: only verdict_gate.py local-side checked this; CI side wasn't.
165	    # A bypassed local hook could land an HMAC-valid verdict under the wrong
166	    # task directory + wrong task scope.
167	    if committed.get("task_id") != args.task_id:
168	        failures.append(
169	            f"committed.task_id={committed.get('task_id')!r} does not match --task-id={args.task_id!r} "
170	            f"(directory placement vs verdict-content mismatch — replay/aliasing detected)"
171	        )
172	    if rerun.get("task_id") != args.task_id and rerun.get("task_id") is not None:
173	        failures.append(
174	            f"rerun.task_id={rerun.get('task_id')!r} does not match --task-id={args.task_id!r}"
175	        )
176	
177	    # 1. Schema validity (committed); rerun is unsigned (Codex direct output) so we
178	    # validate it against a relaxed view that doesn't require hmac_sha256.
179	    # Codex round-2 P1 fix.
180	    ok, msg = _validate_schema(committed, args.schema)
181	    if not ok:
182	        failures.append(f"committed: {msg}")
183	    # For rerun: synthesize an hmac_sha256 placeholder so schema validates structurally.
184	    rerun_for_schema = dict(rerun)
185	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("hmac_sha256", "0" * 64)
186	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("schema_version", "1.0.0")
187	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("canonical_pin_sha", committed.get("canonical_pin_sha", "0" * 64))
188	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("commit_sha", committed.get("commit_sha", "0" * 40))
189	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("diff_sha256", committed.get("diff_sha256", "0" * 64))
190	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("task_id", committed.get("task_id", "unknown"))
191	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("iter", committed.get("iter", 1))
192	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("codex_session_id", "rerun_unsigned")
193	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("model", "gpt-5.5")
194	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("reasoning_effort", "xhigh")
195	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("findings", [])
196	    rerun_for_schema.setdefault("timestamp", "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z")
197	    ok, msg = _validate_schema(rerun_for_schema, args.schema)
198	    if not ok:
199	        failures.append(f"rerun (after defaults): {msg}")
200	
201	    # 2. HMAC (committed only — rerun is unsigned by design, since it's a fresh
202	    #    Codex run on the diff, not a separate commit)
203	    ok, msg = _validate_hmac(committed, hmac_key)
204	    if not ok:
205	        failures.append(f"committed: {msg}")
206	
207	    # 3. Canonical pin
208	    ok, msg = _validate_canonical_pin(committed, args.canonical_pin)
209	    if not ok:
210	        failures.append(f"committed: {msg}")
211	
212	    # 4. Diff SHA cross-check
213	    ok, msg = _validate_diff_sha(committed, args.diff_sha256)
214	    if not ok:
215	        failures.append(f"committed: {msg}")
216	
217	    # 5. Verdict equality
218	    if committed.get("verdict") != "APPROVE":
219	        failures.append(f"committed.verdict={committed.get('verdict')!r}, expected APPROVE")
220	    if rerun.get("verdict") != "APPROVE":
221	        failures.append(f"rerun.verdict={rerun.get('verdict')!r}, expected APPROVE")
222	
223	    # 6. No P0/P1 in rerun
224	    ok, msg = _no_p0_p1(rerun.get("findings", []))
225	    if not ok:
226	        failures.append(f"rerun: {msg}")
227	
228	    # 7. No severity upgrade (rerun cannot escalate findings beyond committed)
229	    ok, msg = _no_severity_upgrade(committed.get("findings", []), rerun.get("findings", []))
230	    if not ok:
231	        failures.append(msg)
232	
233	    if failures:
234	        print("verdict_validator: ❌ BLOCK", file=sys.stderr)
235	        for f in failures:
236	            print(f"  - {f}", file=sys.stderr)
237	        return 1
238	
239	    print(f"verdict_validator: ✅ PASS task={args.task_id} commit={args.commit_sha[:12]}")
240	    return 0
241	
242	
243	if __name__ == "__main__":
244	    sys.exit(main())

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""POLARIS Verdict Gate — shared validator for git pre-commit + Claude PreToolUse hook.
3	
4	Per Plan v13 §C. Validates that every commit has Codex APPROVE verdicts for
5	all tasks implicated by the staged diff. NO PASS-THROUGH BRANCHES.
6	
7	Bootstrap exemption (only during initial setup):
8	  - If state/bootstrap_active exists AND commit message starts with "BOOTSTRAP",
9	    allow commit; log to outputs/audits/codex_audit.jsonl with type="bootstrap_exempt".
10	  - File state/bootstrap_active is deleted at end of §K Step 16 smoke (post-bootstrap).
11	
12	Normal mode:
13	  - Read staged diff (git diff --cached --name-only).
14	  - Map changed files to task IDs via docs/task_acceptance_matrix.yaml.
15	  - For each implicated task, find latest verdict at outputs/audits/verdicts/<task_id>/iter_N.json.
16	  - Validate: schema valid, HMAC valid, verdict == APPROVE, canonical_pin_sha matches.
17	  - Block (exit 1) on any failure.
18	
19	Usage:
20	  python scripts/autoloop/verdict_gate.py [--commit-message-file <path>]
21	    --commit-message-file: path to .git/COMMIT_EDITMSG (used for BOOTSTRAP detection).
22	                            Optional; if omitted, reads from /tmp or skips bootstrap check.
23	"""
24	
25	from __future__ import annotations
26	
27	import argparse
28	import hashlib
29	import hmac
30	import json
31	import os
32	import subprocess
33	import sys
34	from pathlib import Path
35	
36	POLARIS_ROOT = Path("C:/POLARIS").resolve()
37	BOOTSTRAP_FLAG = POLARIS_ROOT / "state" / "bootstrap_active"
38	HMAC_KEY_PATH = POLARIS_ROOT / ".private" / "codex_hmac.key"
39	SCHEMA_PATH = POLARIS_ROOT / "docs" / "schemas" / "codex_verdict.schema.json"
40	VERDICTS_DIR = POLARIS_ROOT / "outputs" / "audits" / "verdicts"
41	AUDIT_LOG = POLARIS_ROOT / "outputs" / "audits" / "codex_audit.jsonl"
42	CANONICAL_PIN = POLARIS_ROOT / "docs" / "canonical_pin.txt"
43	MATRIX_PATH = POLARIS_ROOT / "docs" / "task_acceptance_matrix.yaml"
44	
45	
46	class GateError(Exception):
47	    pass
48	
49	
50	def _staged_files() -> list[str]:
51	    result = subprocess.run(
52	        ["git", "diff", "--cached", "--name-only"],
53	        cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT),
54	        capture_output=True,
55	        text=True, encoding="utf-8",
56	        timeout=10,
57	    )
58	    if result.returncode != 0:
59	        raise GateError(f"git diff failed: {result.stderr}")
60	    return [ln.strip() for ln in result.stdout.splitlines() if ln.strip()]
61	
62	
63	def _staged_diff_sha256() -> str:
64	    """SHA256 of `git diff --cached` output, EXCLUDING verdict files + audit log
65	    (which are part of the same commit but are emitted by the gate workflow itself).
66	
67	    Per Codex round-1 P0-5 fix + P1-2 chicken-and-egg resolution: verdict files at
68	    outputs/audits/verdicts/<task>/iter_*.json AND outputs/audits/codex_audit.jsonl
69	    are written DURING the gate workflow and committed atomically with substrate.
70	    The verdict's `diff_sha256` field covers SUBSTRATE diff only — that's the thing
71	    Codex actually reviewed. Excluding verdict files from the hash lets us atomic-
72	    commit substrate + verdict without circular-hash issues.
73	    """
74	    result = subprocess.run(
75	        ["git", "diff", "--cached", "--",
76	         ":(exclude)outputs/audits/verdicts/**",
77	         ":(exclude)outputs/audits/codex_audit.jsonl",
78	         ":(exclude)outputs/audits/manifests/**"],
79	        cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT),
80	        capture_output=True,
81	        timeout=15,
82	    )
83	    if result.returncode != 0:
84	        raise GateError(f"git diff failed: {result.stderr.decode('utf-8', errors='replace')}")
85	    return hashlib.sha256(result.stdout).hexdigest()
86	
87	
88	def _load_matrix() -> dict:
89	    """Load task_acceptance_matrix.yaml.
90	
91	    Codex round-2 P0 fix: prefer staged blob over working-tree, with HEAD as fallback.
92	    The gate enforces canonical task scope; reading mutable working-tree allows
93	    a malicious or accidental edit to remap globs/task-IDs before commit.
94	
95	    Order:
96	      1. If matrix file is in `git diff --cached --name-only`, read its staged blob
97	      2. Else read from `git show HEAD:docs/task_acceptance_matrix.yaml`
98	      3. Working tree is NEVER trusted as authoritative for the gate.
99	    """
100	    try:
101	        import yaml
102	    except ImportError:
103	        raise GateError("PyYAML required for matrix parsing; pip install pyyaml")
104	
105	    matrix_rel = "docs/task_acceptance_matrix.yaml"
106	
107	    # 1. Staged
108	    try:
109	        result = subprocess.run(
110	            ["git", "diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--", matrix_rel],
111	            cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, encoding="utf-8", timeout=10,
112	        )
113	        if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
114	            blob = subprocess.run(
115	                ["git", "show", f":{matrix_rel}"],
116	                cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, encoding="utf-8", timeout=10,
117	            )
118	            if blob.returncode == 0:
119	                try:
120	                    return yaml.safe_load(blob.stdout) or {}
121	                except Exception as e:
122	                    raise GateError(f"staged matrix parse failed: {e}")
123	    except GateError:
124	        raise
125	    except Exception:
126	        pass
127	
128	    # 2. HEAD
129	    try:
130	        blob = subprocess.run(
131	            ["git", "show", f"HEAD:{matrix_rel}"],
132	            cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, encoding="utf-8", timeout=10,
133	        )
134	        if blob.returncode == 0:
135	            try:
136	                return yaml.safe_load(blob.stdout) or {}
137	            except Exception as e:
138	                raise GateError(f"HEAD matrix parse failed: {e}")
139	    except GateError:
140	        raise
141	    except Exception:
142	        pass
143	
144	    raise GateError("matrix not in staged set or HEAD; canonical task scope undefined")
