147	def _changed_files_to_task_ids(files: list[str], matrix: dict) -> set[str]:
148	    """Map each changed file to one or more task IDs via changed_files_glob.
149	
150	    Resolution: explicit `task_id` field in the task entry takes precedence over
151	    key-derived ID (e.g., `task_bootstrap_smoke` would normally become
152	    `bootstrap.smoke` via the snake_case-to-dot rule, but its `task_id` field
153	    overrides to `bootstrap_smoke`). For numeric phase tasks like `task_0_5`,
154	    the key-derived `0.5` is correct and no override is needed.
155	
156	    Substrate-prep traversal: phase tasks may declare `substrate_prep: [{id, changed_files_glob}]`
157	    entries that author skeleton/prep substrate ahead of a Phase-N user-action finalization.
158	    Each substrate_prep entry has its own task_id (from `id`) and its own changed_files_glob;
159	    these are gated independently of the parent task. Without this traversal, substrate_prep
160	    edits would fall through the gate as "no task implicated" — defeating verdict enforcement.
161	    """
162	    import fnmatch
163	    implicated = set()
164	    for phase_key, phase_val in matrix.items():
165	        if not phase_key.startswith("phase_") or not isinstance(phase_val, dict):
166	            continue
167	        for task_key, task_val in phase_val.items():
168	            if not isinstance(task_val, dict):
169	                continue
170	            task_id = task_val.get("task_id") or task_key.removeprefix("task_").replace("_", ".")
171	            globs = task_val.get("changed_files_glob", []) or []
172	            for glob in globs:
173	                for f in files:
174	                    if fnmatch.fnmatch(f, glob):
175	                        implicated.add(task_id)
176	                        break
177	            substrate_prep = task_val.get("substrate_prep", []) or []
178	            if isinstance(substrate_prep, list):
179	                for sp in substrate_prep:
180	                    if not isinstance(sp, dict):
181	                        continue
182	                    sp_id = sp.get("task_id") or sp.get("id")
183	                    sp_globs = sp.get("changed_files_glob", []) or []
184	                    if not sp_id:
185	                        continue
186	                    for glob in sp_globs:
187	                        for f in files:
188	                            if fnmatch.fnmatch(f, glob):
189	                                implicated.add(sp_id)
190	                                break
191	    return implicated
192	
193	
194	def _latest_verdict(task_id: str) -> dict | None:
195	    """Read the latest iter_*.json verdict for a task.
196	
197	    P1-3 fix: prefer STAGED content over working-tree. If a verdict file exists
198	    on disk but is NOT staged (or is staged with different bytes), the file isn't
199	    actually being committed and would not be visible to CI. Require staging.
200	
201	    Order:
202	      1. If a verdict file is in `git diff --cached --name-only`, read its
203	         staged blob via `git show :<path>` (= contents that will be committed).
204	      2. Else if file is in HEAD, read via `git show HEAD:<path>`.
205	      3. Else None.
206	    """
207	    verdict_glob = f"outputs/audits/verdicts/{task_id}/"
208	
209	    # 1. Check staged set
210	    try:
211	        result = subprocess.run(
212	            ["git", "diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--", verdict_glob],
213	            cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, encoding="utf-8", timeout=10,
214	        )
215	        staged = sorted(
216	            ln for ln in result.stdout.splitlines()
217	            if ln.endswith(".json") and "iter_" in ln
218	        )
219	        if staged:
220	            blob = subprocess.run(
221	                ["git", "show", f":{staged[-1]}"],
222	                cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, encoding="utf-8", timeout=10,
223	            )
224	            if blob.returncode == 0:
225	                try:
226	                    return json.loads(blob.stdout)
227	                except Exception:
228	                    return None
229	    except Exception:
230	        pass
231	
232	    # 2. Fall back to HEAD
233	    try:
234	        ls = subprocess.run(
235	            ["git", "ls-tree", "--name-only", "HEAD", verdict_glob],
236	            cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, encoding="utf-8", timeout=10,
237	        )
238	        head_files = sorted(
239	            ln for ln in ls.stdout.splitlines()
240	            if ln.endswith(".json") and "iter_" in ln
241	        )
242	        if head_files:
243	            blob = subprocess.run(
244	                ["git", "show", f"HEAD:{head_files[-1]}"],
245	                cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, encoding="utf-8", timeout=10,
246	            )
247	            if blob.returncode == 0:
248	                return json.loads(blob.stdout)
249	    except Exception:
250	        pass
251	
252	    return None
253

255	def _load_schema() -> dict:
256	    """Load verdict schema from staged-or-HEAD (Codex round-2 P0 fix; same trust model as matrix)."""
257	    schema_rel = "docs/schemas/codex_verdict.schema.json"
258	    try:
259	        result = subprocess.run(
260	            ["git", "diff", "--cached", "--name-only", "--", schema_rel],
261	            cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, encoding="utf-8", timeout=10,
262	        )
263	        if result.returncode == 0 and result.stdout.strip():
264	            blob = subprocess.run(
265	                ["git", "show", f":{schema_rel}"],
266	                cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, encoding="utf-8", timeout=10,
267	            )
268	            if blob.returncode == 0:
269	                return json.loads(blob.stdout)
270	    except Exception:
271	        pass
272	    blob = subprocess.run(
273	        ["git", "show", f"HEAD:{schema_rel}"],
274	        cwd=str(POLARIS_ROOT), capture_output=True, text=True, encoding="utf-8", timeout=10,
275	    )
276	    if blob.returncode == 0:
277	        return json.loads(blob.stdout)
278	    raise GateError("schema not in staged set or HEAD")

281	def _validate_schema(verdict: dict) -> tuple[bool, str]:
282	    try:
283	        import jsonschema
284	        schema = _load_schema()
285	        jsonschema.validate(verdict, schema)
286	        return True, "schema ok"
287	    except ImportError:
288	        # Best-effort fallback — check required fields manually
289	        required = {"task_id", "iter", "codex_session_id", "verdict",
290	                    "canonical_pin_sha", "hmac_sha256", "findings", "timestamp"}
291	        missing = required - set(verdict.keys())
292	        if missing:
293	            return False, f"missing fields: {missing}"
294	        return True, "schema ok (jsonschema fallback)"
295	    except Exception as e:
296	        return False, f"schema validation failed: {e}"
297	
298	
299	def _canonical_serialize(verdict: dict) -> bytes:
300	    """Canonical serialization for HMAC: sorted keys, no spaces, drop hmac_sha256."""
301	    payload = {k: v for k, v in verdict.items() if k != "hmac_sha256"}
302	    return json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
303	
304	
305	def _validate_hmac(verdict: dict, key: bytes) -> tuple[bool, str]:
306	    expected = verdict.get("hmac_sha256")
307	    if not expected:
308	        return False, "verdict missing hmac_sha256"
309	    payload = _canonical_serialize(verdict)
310	    actual = hmac.new(key, payload, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
311	    if not hmac.compare_digest(actual, expected):
312	        return False, f"hmac mismatch: expected {expected[:12]}, computed {actual[:12]}"
313	    return True, "hmac ok"
314	
315	
316	def _validate_canonical_pin(verdict: dict) -> tuple[bool, str]:
317	    expected = verdict.get("canonical_pin_sha")
318	    if not expected:
319	        return False, "verdict missing canonical_pin_sha"
320	    actual = hashlib.sha256(CANONICAL_PIN.read_bytes()).hexdigest()
321	    if actual != expected:
322	        return False, f"canonical_pin_sha mismatch: verdict={expected[:12]}, current={actual[:12]}"
323	    return True, "canonical pin ok"
324

326	def _is_bootstrap(commit_msg_path: str | None) -> bool:
327	    """Bootstrap exemption per Plan v13 §K — auto-expires after MAX commits.
328	
329	    Codex round-1 P0-2 partial fix: hard cap on bootstrap commit count. Prevents
330	    indefinite exemption-bypass even if bootstrap_active flag persists or is
331	    recreated. Full prevention requires GPG-signed flag (decision #13 not yet
332	    landed); this is the strongest fix available pre-GPG.
333	    """
334	    BOOTSTRAP_MAX_COMMITS = 20  # generous; actual count expected ~10-12
335	
336	    if not BOOTSTRAP_FLAG.exists():
337	        return False
338	
339	    # Hard count limit
340	    count_path = POLARIS_ROOT / "state" / "bootstrap_commit_count"
341	    try:
342	        count = int(count_path.read_text(encoding="utf-8").strip())
343	    except Exception:
344	        count = 0
345	    if count >= BOOTSTRAP_MAX_COMMITS:
346	        # Exemption auto-expired; force-delete flag to make state visible
347	        try:
348	            BOOTSTRAP_FLAG.unlink()
349	        except Exception:
350	            pass
351	        return False
352	
353	    if not commit_msg_path:
354	        return False
355	    try:
356	        msg = Path(commit_msg_path).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
357	    except Exception:
358	        return False
359	    lines = [ln for ln in msg.lstrip().splitlines() if ln.strip()]
360	    if not lines or not lines[0].startswith("BOOTSTRAP"):
361	        return False
362	
363	    # Increment count atomically (best-effort; mtime-protect)
364	    try:
365	        count_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
366	        count_path.write_text(str(count + 1), encoding="utf-8")
367	    except Exception:
368	        pass
369	
370	    return True
371	
372	
373	def _audit_log_event(event: dict) -> None:
374	    """Append to codex_audit.jsonl with prev_sha chain (best-effort)."""
375	    try:
376	        AUDIT_LOG.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
377	        prev_sha = None
378	        if AUDIT_LOG.exists():
379	            with open(AUDIT_LOG, "rb") as f:
380	                f.seek(0, 2)
381	                size = f.tell()
382	                # Read last KB
383	                f.seek(max(0, size - 4096))
384	                tail = f.read().decode("utf-8", errors="replace")
385	            for line in reversed(tail.strip().splitlines()):
386	                try:
387	                    last = json.loads(line)
388	                    prev_sha = last.get("entry_sha")
389	                    break
390	                except Exception:
391	                    continue
392	        event["prev_sha"] = prev_sha
393	        # Compute entry_sha excluding entry_sha field itself
394	        payload = {k: v for k, v in event.items() if k != "entry_sha"}
395	        ser = json.dumps(payload, sort_keys=True, separators=(",", ":"), ensure_ascii=False).encode("utf-8")
396	        event["entry_sha"] = hashlib.sha256(ser).hexdigest()
397	        with open(AUDIT_LOG, "a", encoding="utf-8") as f:
398	            f.write(json.dumps(event, ensure_ascii=False) + "\n")
399	    except Exception:
400	        pass  # never let audit log failure block a legitimate gate decision
401	
402	
403	def main() -> int:
404	    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
405	    parser.add_argument("--commit-message-file", default=None)
406	    args = parser.parse_args()
407	
408	    import time
409	    timestamp = time.strftime("%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ", time.gmtime())
410	
411	    # Bootstrap exemption check
412	    if _is_bootstrap(args.commit_message_file):
413	        _audit_log_event({
414	            "ts": timestamp,
415	            "type": "bootstrap_exempt",
416	            "commit_message_file": args.commit_message_file,
417	        })
418	        print("verdict_gate: BOOTSTRAP commit exempted (state/bootstrap_active present).", file=sys.stderr)
419	        return 0
420	
421	    # Load matrix — Codex round-2 P0 fix: missing matrix → BLOCK (was: allow with warn).
422	    # Missing matrix means the canonical task scope is undefined; allowing commits
423	    # in that state lets arbitrary changes through with no task implication check.
424	    if not MATRIX_PATH.exists():
425	        print("verdict_gate: BLOCK matrix missing at " + str(MATRIX_PATH), file=sys.stderr)
426	        print(
427	            "Per Plan v13 §A: docs/task_acceptance_matrix.yaml is canonical and pinned. "
428	            "Restore from `git show HEAD:docs/task_acceptance_matrix.yaml` before retrying.",
429	            file=sys.stderr,
430	        )
431	        return 1
432	
433	    try:
434	        matrix = _load_matrix()
435	    except GateError as e:
436	        print(f"verdict_gate: ERROR matrix unparseable: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
437	        return 1
438	
439	    files = _staged_files()
440	    if not files:
441	        return 0  # empty commit, nothing to gate
442	
443	    implicated = _changed_files_to_task_ids(files, matrix)
444	    if not implicated:
445	        # Codex round-1 P0-6 fix: NO PASS-THROUGH on no-task-implicated.
446	        # Block by default. Allow only if commit message contains explicit
447	        # `infrastructure-only:` line.
448	        # Architecture: pre-commit hook can't reliably read commit message;
449	        # message-dependent check fires from commit-msg hook (which DOES pass
450	        # --commit-message-file). When --commit-message-file is NOT provided
451	        # (= we're in pre-commit stage), DEFER message-dependent decision to
452	        # commit-msg hook by passing through. Substantive HMAC/schema/canonical
453	        # /diff_sha verdict checks still run unconditionally.
454	        if not args.commit_message_file:
455	            print(
456	                "verdict_gate: WARN no task implicated by staged files; "
457	                "deferring infrastructure-only check to commit-msg hook.",
458	                file=sys.stderr,
459	            )
460	            _audit_log_event({
461	                "ts": timestamp,
462	                "type": "deferred_to_commit_msg",
463	                "files": files,
464	            })
465	            return 0
466	        commit_msg = ""
467	        try:
468	            commit_msg = Path(args.commit_message_file).read_text(encoding="utf-8")
469	        except Exception:
470	            pass
471	        if "infrastructure-only:" not in commit_msg:
472	            print(
473	                "verdict_gate: BLOCK no task implicated by staged files. Per Plan v13 §C,\n"
474	                "every commit MUST be tied to a task in docs/task_acceptance_matrix.yaml\n"
475	                "via changed_files_glob. If this is genuinely infrastructure-only (build\n"
476	                "deps, runner config), add `infrastructure-only: <one-line-justification>`\n"
477	                "to the commit message.\n"
478	                f"Staged files: {files}",
479	                file=sys.stderr,
480	            )
481	            _audit_log_event({
482	                "ts": timestamp,
483	                "type": "gate_blocked_no_task",
484	                "files": files,
485	            })
486	            return 1
487	        # Infrastructure-only path
488	        print("verdict_gate: ALLOW (infrastructure-only commit message present)", file=sys.stderr)
489	        _audit_log_event({
490	            "ts": timestamp,
491	            "type": "infrastructure_only_allow",
492	            "files": files,
493	            "commit_msg_excerpt": commit_msg[:500],
494	        })
495	        return 0
496	
497	    # P0-5 fix: compute staged diff SHA for cross-check against verdict.diff_sha256
498	    try:
499	        diff_sha = _staged_diff_sha256()
500	    except GateError as e:
501	        print(f"verdict_gate: BLOCK staged-diff hashing failed: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
502	        return 1
503	
504	    # Load HMAC key
505	    if not HMAC_KEY_PATH.exists():
506	        print(f"verdict_gate: BLOCK no HMAC key at {HMAC_KEY_PATH}", file=sys.stderr)
507	        return 1
508	    try:
509	        hmac_key = HMAC_KEY_PATH.read_bytes().strip()
510	    except Exception as e:
511	        print(f"verdict_gate: BLOCK HMAC key read failed: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
512	        return 1
513	
514	    # Validate verdict for each implicated task
515	    failed = []
516	    for task_id in sorted(implicated):
517	        # Plan v13 §F path 3 (halt-resolution Phase-N-PARTIAL-honest): if a halt-resolution
518	        # marker exists for this task, the halt-resolution IS the documented decision and
519	        # the gate accepts the staged commit. The substrate ships with documented
520	        # Phase-N-deferred gaps; future Phase entry deletes the marker + re-iterates.
521	        halt_marker = POLARIS_ROOT / "outputs" / "audits" / "halt_resolutions" / f"{task_id}_halt.md"
522	        if halt_marker.is_file():
523	            print(
524	                f"verdict_gate: ALLOW task {task_id} via halt-resolution marker "
525	                f"({halt_marker.relative_to(POLARIS_ROOT).as_posix()}) — Plan v13 §F path 3.",
526	                file=sys.stderr,
527	            )
528	            _audit_log_event({
529	                "ts": timestamp,
530	                "type": "halt_resolution_allow",
531	                "task_id": task_id,
532	                "halt_marker": halt_marker.relative_to(POLARIS_ROOT).as_posix(),
533	            })
534	            continue
535	        verdict = _latest_verdict(task_id)
536	        if verdict is None:
537	            failed.append(f"{task_id}: no verdict file at outputs/audits/verdicts/{task_id}/iter_N.json")
538	            continue
539	        ok, msg = _validate_schema(verdict)
540	        if not ok:
541	            failed.append(f"{task_id}: schema invalid — {msg}")
542	            continue
543	        ok, msg = _validate_hmac(verdict, hmac_key)
544	        if not ok:
545	            failed.append(f"{task_id}: HMAC invalid — {msg}")
546	            continue
547	        ok, msg = _validate_canonical_pin(verdict)
548	        if not ok:
549	            failed.append(f"{task_id}: canonical pin mismatch — {msg}")
550	            continue
551	        # P0-5 fix: verdict.diff_sha256 MUST match staged diff. Pre-bootstrap backfill
552	        # verdicts have a sentinel diff_sha256 (sha256 of "pre_bootstrap_backfill") and
553	        # are not commit-linked, so we skip this check for pre_bootstrap_backfill session.
554	        if verdict.get("codex_session_id", "").startswith("pre_bootstrap_backfill"):
555	            pass  # backfill verdicts are honest synthetic; not commit-linked
556	        else:
557	            verdict_diff_sha = verdict.get("diff_sha256", "")
558	            if verdict_diff_sha != diff_sha:
559	                failed.append(
560	                    f"{task_id}: diff_sha256 mismatch (replay defense) — "
561	                    f"verdict={verdict_diff_sha[:12]}, staged={diff_sha[:12]}"
562	                )
563	                continue
564	        if verdict.get("verdict") != "APPROVE":
565	            failed.append(f"{task_id}: verdict is {verdict.get('verdict')!r}, not APPROVE")
566	            continue
567	        # Codex round-2 P0 fix: verdict.task_id MUST match the implicated task.
568	        # Otherwise an APPROVE for task A could be replayed against task B's diff.
569	        verdict_task_id = verdict.get("task_id", "")
570	        if verdict_task_id != task_id:
571	            failed.append(
572	                f"{task_id}: verdict.task_id={verdict_task_id!r} does not match implicated task — "
573	                f"replay/aliasing detected"
574	            )
575	            continue
576	
577	    if failed:
578	        print("verdict_gate: BLOCK — Codex verdict gate failed for staged commit:", file=sys.stderr)
579	        for f in failed:
580	            print(f"  - {f}", file=sys.stderr)
581	        print(
582	            "\nPer Plan v13 §C: every commit requires APPROVE verdict for each "
583	            "implicated task. Run the orchestrator (or `codex exec` manually) "
584	            "to obtain a verdict before re-attempting commit.",
585	            file=sys.stderr,
586	        )
587	        _audit_log_event({
588	            "ts": timestamp,
589	            "type": "gate_blocked",
590	            "implicated": list(implicated),
591	            "failures": failed,
592	            "files": files,
593	        })
594	        return 1
595	
596	    # All implicated tasks have valid APPROVE
597	    _audit_log_event({
598	        "ts": timestamp,
599	        "type": "gate_passed",
600	        "implicated": list(implicated),
601	        "files": files,
602	    })
603	    return 0
604	
605	
606	if __name__ == "__main__":
607	    sys.exit(main())

1	"""I-bench-001 — v6 benchmark proof-package harness. Deterministic internal scoring per docs/benchmark/scoring_rubric.md. Always emits all 6 v6 canonical dimensions; suite.score_dimensions filters composite (others get raw=0.0)."""
2	
3	from __future__ import annotations
4	
5	import argparse
6	import json
7	import sys
8	from pathlib import Path
9	
10	sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / "src"))
11	
12	from polaris_v6.benchmark.coverage_scorer import score_response_coverage
13	from polaris_v6.benchmark.schema import (
14	    BenchmarkScore, BenchmarkSuiteDesign, CompetingSystem, DimensionScore,
15	    ScoreDimension, SystemAnswer,
16	)
17	
18	
19	_REFUSAL = ("i cannot", "i can't")
20	_ALL_DIMS: tuple[ScoreDimension, ...] = (
21	    "factual_accuracy", "citation_health", "frame_coverage",
22	    "contradiction_handling", "refusal_calibration", "user_traceability",
23	)
24	
25	
26	def _is_refusal(text: str) -> bool:
27	    low = text.lower()
28	    return any(m in low for m in _REFUSAL)
29	
30	
31	def _score_one(q, sys_id: CompetingSystem, response: str,
32	               wanted: list[ScoreDimension]) -> BenchmarkScore:
33	    coverage = score_response_coverage(q, response)
34	    cit = response.lower().count("http")
35	    refused = _is_refusal(response)
36	    pool: dict[ScoreDimension, tuple[float, str]] = {
37	        "factual_accuracy": (coverage, f"coverage={coverage:.2f}"),
38	        "citation_health": (min(1.0, cit / 5), f"{cit} citations"),
39	        "frame_coverage": (coverage, "internal deterministic"),
40	        "contradiction_handling": (0.5, "internal deterministic"),
41	        "refusal_calibration": (
42	            1.0 if refused == bool(q.expected_refusals) else 0.5,
43	            f"refused={refused} expected={bool(q.expected_refusals)}",
44	        ),
45	        "user_traceability": (min(1.0, cit / 3), f"{cit} cites"),
46	    }
47	    sel = set(wanted)
48	    dims = [
49	        DimensionScore(dimension=d, raw=pool[d][0], rationale=pool[d][1])
50	        if d in sel
51	        else DimensionScore(dimension=d, raw=0.0, rationale="not requested by suite")
52	        for d in _ALL_DIMS
53	    ]
54	    raws = [pool[d][0] for d in wanted if d in pool]
55	    composite = sum(raws) / max(1, len(raws))
56	    return BenchmarkScore(
57	        question_id=q.question_id, system=sys_id, dimensions=dims,
58	        composite=composite, scored_by="automated",
59	    )
60	
61	
62	def main(argv: list[str] | None = None) -> int:
63	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser()
64	    p.add_argument("--suite-design", required=True, type=Path)

69	    suite = BenchmarkSuiteDesign.model_validate_json(

73	
74	    answers, scores = [], []

1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""CI gate — block secret-bearing / non-slim .codex/** artifacts (I-sec-001 #535).
3	
4	Invoked by ``.github/workflows/codex_artifact_gate.yml``. That workflow uses
5	``pull_request_target``, so this script runs from the trusted base ref; the
6	PR's own copy is never executed. PR content is inspected as data only.
7	
8	For every changed ``.codex/**`` path in the PR (git status A/M/R/C — deletions
9	are ignored):
10	  * denylist — raw ``codex exec`` transcript filename patterns are rejected
11	    (verdict-only commit policy);
12	  * allowlist — only the slim per-issue artifact filenames are permitted;
13	  * content  — verdict/audit artifacts must validate as schema-bounded slim
14	    blocks via ``extract_codex_verdict.py validate``;
15	  * secret scan — every changed ``.codex/**`` file is scanned with
16	    ``scan_for_secrets.py --strict``.
17	
18	Fast-passes (exit 0) when the PR changes no ``.codex/**`` path — so the gate
19	can be a *required* status check without deadlocking non-.codex PRs.
20	"""
21	from __future__ import annotations
22	
23	import argparse
24	import re
25	import subprocess
26	import sys
27	from pathlib import Path
28	
29	# Raw codex-exec transcripts must never be committed (verdict-only policy).
30	DENY_RE = re.compile(
31	    r"(codex_.*review.*\.txt"
32	    r"|codex_.*audit_iter.*\.txt"
33	    r"|codex_.*_iter[0-9]+\.txt)$",
34	    re.I,
35	)
36	# Slim per-issue artifacts permitted under .codex/<issue_id>/.
37	ALLOWED_BASENAMES = {
38	    "brief.md", "diff_brief.md", "codex_brief_verdict.txt",
39	    "codex_diff_audit.txt", "codex_diff.patch",
40	}
41	FORCE_APPROVE_RE = re.compile(r".*_iter5_force_approve\.txt$")
42	# Artifacts whose content must be a schema-bounded slim verdict.
43	VALIDATE_RE = re.compile(
44	    r"(codex_brief_verdict\.txt|codex_diff_audit\.txt|.*_iter5_force_approve\.txt)$"
45	)
46	
47	
48	def changed_codex_paths(pr_dir: Path, base_branch: str) -> list[tuple[str, str]]:
49	    """[(status, path)] for changed .codex/** paths; deletions dropped."""
50	    subprocess.run(
51	        ["git", "-C", str(pr_dir), "fetch", "--no-tags", "--quiet",
52	         "origin", base_branch],
53	        check=False, capture_output=True,
54	    )
55	    r = subprocess.run(
56	        ["git", "-C", str(pr_dir), "diff", "--name-status",
57	         f"origin/{base_branch}...HEAD", "--", ".codex/"],
58	        capture_output=True, text=True, check=True,
59	    )
60	    out: list[tuple[str, str]] = []
61	    for line in r.stdout.splitlines():
62	        parts = line.split("\t")
63	        if len(parts) < 2:
64	            continue
65	        status = parts[0][0]
66	        if status == "D":
67	            continue
68	        out.append((status, parts[-1]))  # dest path (R/C put dest last)
69	    return out
70	
71	
72	def main() -> int:
73	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser()
74	    ap.add_argument("--pr-checkout", required=True,
75	                    help="dir holding the PR head checkout (data only)")
76	    ap.add_argument("--base-branch", default="polaris")
77	    ap.add_argument("--scanner", required=True,
78	                    help="path to the trusted base scan_for_secrets.py")
79	    ap.add_argument("--validator", required=True,
80	                    help="path to the trusted base extract_codex_verdict.py")
81	    args = ap.parse_args()
82	
83	    pr = Path(args.pr_checkout)
84	    changed = changed_codex_paths(pr, args.base_branch)
85	    if not changed:
86	        print("codex-artifact-gate: no .codex/** changes — PASS (fast)")
87	        return 0
88	
89	    failures: list[str] = []
90	    for status, path in changed:
91	        base = Path(path).name
92	        fpath = pr / path
93	        # `.codex/<issue_id>/<file>` is a per-issue artifact dir (>= 3 path
94	        # parts); `.codex/<file>` is a top-level protocol/checklist doc with
95	        # arbitrary names. The denylist + secret scan apply everywhere; the
96	        # slim-artifact allowlist applies ONLY inside issue dirs (codex diff
97	        # review iter-1 P2 — else AUDIT_CYCLE_PROTOCOL.md etc. would fail).
98	        parts = path.replace("\\", "/").split("/")
99	        in_issue_dir = len(parts) >= 3
100	        if not FORCE_APPROVE_RE.match(base):
101	            if DENY_RE.search(base):
102	                failures.append(
103	                    f"{path}: raw codex exec transcript must not be committed "
104	                    "— commit only the slim verdict (verdict-only policy, "
105	                    "I-sec-001 #535)")
106	                continue
107	            if in_issue_dir and base not in ALLOWED_BASENAMES:
108	                failures.append(
109	                    f"{path}: filename not on the .codex/<id>/ allowlist "
110	                    f"{sorted(ALLOWED_BASENAMES)} (+ *_iter5_force_approve.txt)")
111	                continue
112	        if not fpath.is_file():
113	            continue  # rename edge with content elsewhere — nothing to scan
114	        if VALIDATE_RE.search(base):
115	            v = subprocess.run(
116	                [sys.executable, args.validator, "validate", str(fpath)],
117	                capture_output=True, text=True)
118	            if v.returncode != 0:
119	                failures.append(
120	                    f"{path}: not a schema-bounded slim verdict — "
121	                    f"{(v.stdout + ' ' + v.stderr).strip()}")
122	        s = subprocess.run(
123	            [sys.executable, args.scanner, str(fpath), "--strict"],
124	            capture_output=True, text=True)
125	        if s.returncode != 0:
126	            failures.append(
127	                f"{path}: secret pattern detected by scan_for_secrets")
128	
129	    if failures:
130	        print("codex-artifact-gate: FAIL")
131	        for f in failures:
132	            print(f"  - {f}")
133	        return 1
134	    print(f"codex-artifact-gate: PASS — {len(changed)} .codex/** path(s) clean")
135	    return 0
136	
137	
138	if __name__ == "__main__":
139	    sys.exit(main())
